title: 1.2: Identifies that interface selection is determined by routing table lookup for the destination (e.g., interface chosen via route lookup, uses conf.route.route() for destination, packet.route() determines interface)
1.3: Reports that Scapy resolves the gateway MAC address for external destinations (e.g., queries gateway instead of final destination, resolves next-hop MAC, performs ARP for gateway IP)
1.4: Reports that ARP caching prevents repeated requests when sending same request twice (e.g., second packet uses cached MAC, no ARP sent for cached entries, retrieves MAC from cache)
1.5: Identifies the warning message when no route exists as the send() completes(e.g., "No route found (no default route?)", routing failure warning, no-route warning text)
1.6: Reports the fallback behavior when no route exists (e.g., returns loopback interface, uses lo with 0.0.0.0 addresses, falls back to loopback)
